briefing.opening: Good morning. We will now begin the climate briefing for May 24, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Global Heatwave Warnings and Deepening Ecosystem Threats
sections.content: According to news reported today, India is experiencing severe casualties, with 37 heatstroke deaths due to a deadly heatwave reaching 48.2 degrees Celsius [3]. The UK also recorded its hottest day of the year, with temperatures in Kent reaching 30.5C, exceeding 30C in May for the first time since 2012, prompting an Amber Health Alert and warnings of even more extreme heatwaves [26]. Such climate change poses a serious threat to global ecosystems, with scientists warning that thousands of plant species could be destroyed by the end of this century [23].

sections.title: 🌍 International Community Reaffirms Climate Responsibility Amidst Domestic Policy Controversy
sections.content: The international community clearly recognizes responding to the climate crisis as an obligation, not an option. On the 20th, the UN General Assembly welcomed and urged the implementation of the International Court of Justice (ICJ)'s advisory opinion on climate change, reaffirming that climate crisis response is a legal obligation for each nation [2, 19]. However, domestically, despite emphasizing 'carbon neutrality,' reports indicate that next year's climate response budget has been cut by 16% compared to plans, raising concerns about policy consistency [7]. Meanwhile, analyses suggest that the Korean steel industry is being caught in the crossfire due to the European Union (EU)'s measures to curb Chinese steel, indicating that the government's negotiating power and the development of eco-friendly products will determine its future [20].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Facing Challenges
sections.content: Domestic efforts for renewable energy transition are actively underway. Solar power projects totaling 3.12GW are becoming visible in the Jeonnam region, with concrete achievements such as obtaining a 400MW power generation business license in Jangheung [17]. Furthermore, the Ministry of Climate is accelerating the development of 'next-generation energy storage systems (ESS)' to reduce the intermittency of renewable energy, attempting to solve technical challenges [16]. However, policy confusion is arising as energy pledges from both ruling and opposition parties show a stark contrast between nuclear power and renewable energy, with a pledge for nuclear power plant construction being proposed in the Saemangeum region, known as a hub for renewable energy [13, 14]. The fact that the renewable energy self-sufficiency rate in a specific industrial city is merely 3.2%, and candidates remain silent on transmission grid construction damage despite supporting the 'local production, local consumption' principle for renewable energy, illustrates the realistic difficulties of the transition process [15, 18].

sections.title: 🌱 Spreading Diverse Efforts for Carbon Neutrality Realization
sections.content: Efforts by various stakeholders to achieve carbon neutrality in response to climate change are spreading both domestically and internationally. Domestically, 'Tamgu 2' has been launched to protect resources in the West Sea amidst climate change [1], Geoje and Tongyeong mayoral candidates agree on carbon neutrality and renewable energy expansion [4], and Geoje City is promoting carbon neutrality practices at university festivals, raising awareness in local communities [9]. Plans are also underway to provide benefits of up to 100,000 won for using bicycles and tumblers to encourage individual participation [5, 8], and Hansung Motor planted 600 Korean fir trees at Hoengseong Forest Retreat as part of its carbon neutrality efforts [11]. Additionally, the Korea Chamber of Commerce and Industry and Korea Southern Power have signed a business agreement for cooperation in carbon neutrality and energy transition, demonstrating active participation from the corporate sector [6, 12].

briefing.closing: As confirmed in today's briefing, climate change is an issue directly linked to human survival, requiring simultaneous international obligations and diverse efforts both domestically and abroad. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres. Reuters-Yonhap News. “The world’s highest court has decided, and today the UN General Assembly has responded.” The message that climate crisis response is not a 'choice' for individual nations but a 'legal obligation' to be upheld by the international community was reaffirmed at the UN General Assembly. On the 20th (local time) in New York, USA, the UN General Assembly welcomed and urged the implementation of the International Court of Justice (ICJ)'s advisory opinion on climate change,...

summary.phenomenon: At the UN General Assembly held on the 20th (local time) in New York, USA, the International Court of Justice (ICJ)'s advisory opinion on climate change was welcomed and its implementation urged, reaffirming that climate crisis response is a legal obligation for each nation.
summary.outlook: As climate crisis response is emphasized as a legal obligation to be upheld by the international community, efforts by each nation to address climate change are expected to intensify.

articles.description: European Parliament raises import steel tariffs from 25% to 50%, cuts duty-free import quotas by nearly half. Measures aim to block massive influx of low-priced Chinese products. “Government diplomatic efforts crucial for quota allocation.” POSCO and Hyundai Steel accelerate eco-friendly product development. Image of an electric arc furnace at Hyundai Steelworks in Dangjin, Chungnam, used in a hybrid electric arc furnace-blast furnace process. Provided by Hyundai Steel. The European Union (EU) has introduced tariffs to prevent a massive influx of low-priced Chinese steel products...

summary.phenomenon: The European Union (EU) increased import tariffs on steel from 25% to 50% and nearly halved duty-free import quotas to prevent a massive influx of low-priced Chinese steel products. This has inadvertently impacted the South Korean steel industry.
summary.cause: The EU took measures to prevent the large-scale influx of low-priced Chinese steel products.
summary.outlook: The South Korean steel industry will need to secure its future through the government's diplomatic efforts in quota allocation and the development of eco-friendly products by companies like POSCO and Hyundai Steel.

articles.original_title: UK records hottest day of year as forecasters warn of more extreme heat
articles.description: Temperature reaches 30.5C in Kent as amber health alerts issued before bank holiday temperatures riseThe UK has recorded its hottest day of the year so far, with temperatures reaching 30.5C in Kent as forecasters warned more extreme heat could follow over the bank holiday weekend.The temperature in Frittenden also marked the first time since 2012 the UK has reached 30C in May, according to the Met Office. Continue reading...

summary.phenomenon: The UK recorded its hottest day of the year so far, with temperatures reaching 30.5C in Kent. This marks the first time since 2012 that UK temperatures have reached 30C in May, and amber health alerts have been issued.
summary.outlook: Forecasters warned that more extreme heat could follow over the bank holiday weekend.
